name: sqlite3-parser

metadata.name: sqlite3-parser
metadata.summary: The SQLite SQL parser for python. Uses SQLite's original lexer

documentation:
## Example of usage :

SQLite parsing :

```python

"""Showcase the SQLite parser."""
from sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer import SQLiteFullParseResultType, SQLiteParser
from sqlite3_parser.sqlite_tree import SQLiteTree

query: str = """--sql
with recursive hierarchy (e_id, s_id, name, levels) as (
    select employees.employee_id
    , employees.reports_to
    , employees.last_name || ' ' || employees.first_name
    , 0
    from employees
union
    select hierarchy.s_id, employees.reports_to
    , hierarchy.name || ' > ' || employees.last_name || ' ' || employees.first_name
    , hierarchy.levels + 1
    from hierarchy
    join employees
    on employees.employee_id = hierarchy.s_id
    where employees.employee_id is not null
)
select hierarchy.name as employee_and_hierarchy
    , max(hierarchy.levels) over () + 1 - hierarchy.levels as hierarchical_level
from hierarchy
where s_id is null
order by hierarchical_level desc;
;

"""

parser: SQLiteParser  = SQLiteParser(None)  # no cache ; slower
result: SQLiteFullParseResultType = parser.parse(query)
sqlite_tree: SQLiteTree = result.rebuilt_parse_tree

print(sqlite_tree[0, 1, 0, 2, 1].content)  # subtree
# (e_id, s_id, name, levels)

leaf = sqlite_tree[0, 1, 0, 2, 1, 1, 4, 0, 0, 0]
print(f"{leaf.is_leaf=}, {leaf.element=}, {leaf.content=}")  # leaf
# leaf.is_leaf=True, leaf.element=<TokenType.TK_ID: 'ID'>, leaf.content='name'

```

General use :

```python
from enum import Enum

from parser import AutoCalculatingToken, Unordered, parse
from parser.tree.classic_tree import ClassicTree

grammar = """
start: q r s
q: AqA
| A
r: C*
t: AtA
| A A
| B B
| BtB
s: (C t)+

%declare A B C

"""

input = """
    D
    DAAAAACCDCCABABBBBABACBB
    D
"""
# the first active token will therefore start at position 9 as
# tokens will all have a single character as content.

class Terminals(Enum):
    A = "A"
    B = "B"
    C = "C"
    D = "D"
    S = " "
    NL = "\n"

class Rules(Enum):
    start = "start"
    q = "q"
    r = "r"
    s = "s"
    t = "t"


full_tokens_list: list[
    AutoCalculatingToken[Terminals, None]] = []

for i, c in enumerate(input):
    full_tokens_list.append(AutoCalculatingToken(
        content=c,
        start_position=i,
        stop_position=i+1,  # len(c) is always 1 here
        token_type=Terminals(c),
        previous=full_tokens_list[-1] if i else None,
        data=None,
    ))


ignored_terminals = frozenset([
    Terminals.D,
    Terminals.S,
    Terminals.NL,
])

tree: ClassicTree[Terminals, Rules, None, None, None] = parse(
    full_tokens_list, ignored_terminals, Rules, grammar)

print(tree.pretty(max_compensation=4, content_size=22))

print("\n")

for node in tree:
    match node:
        case ClassicTree(Rules.t, _, _, Unordered(Rules.t)):
            print(f"Node starting at position {node.start_position} and ending at {node.stop_position} is a nested {Rules.t}.")
```

Output :
```
start                                   column   5 from    12 :   AAAAACCDCCABABBBBABACB   ->    35
|*  q                                   column   5 from    12 :   AAAAA                    ->    17
|*  |   A                               column   5 from    12 :   A                        ->    13
|*  |   q                               column   6 from    13 :   AAA                      ->    16
|*  |   |   A                           column   6 from    13 :   A                        ->    14
|*  |   |   q                           column   7 from    14 :   A                        ->    15
|*  |   |   |   A                       column   7 from    14 :   A                        ->    15
|*  |   |   A                           column   8 from    15 :   A                        ->    16
|*  |   A                               column   9 from    16 :   A                        ->    17
|*  r                                   column  10 from    17 :   CCDC                     ->    21
|*  |   C                               column  10 from    17 :   C                        ->    18
|*  |   C                               column  11 from    18 :   C                        ->    19
|*  |   C                               column  13 from    20 :   C                        ->    21
|*  s                                   column  14 from    21 :   CABABBBBABACBB           ->    35
|*  |   C                               column  14 from    21 :   C                        ->    22
|*  |   t                               column  15 from    22 :   ABABBBBABA               ->    32
|*  |   |   A                           column  15 from    22 :   A                        ->    23
|*  |   |   t                           column  16 from    23 :   BABBBBAB                 ->    31
|*  |   |   |   B                       column  16 from    23 :   B                        ->    24
|*  |   |   |   t                       column  17 from    24 :   ABBBBA                   ->    30
|*  |   |   |   |*  A                   column  17 from    24 :   A                        ->    25
|*  |   |   |   |*  t                   column  18 from    25 :   BBBB                     ->    29
|*  |   |   |   |*  |   B               column  18 from    25 :   B                        ->    26
|*  |   |   |   |*  |   t               column  19 from    26 :   BB                       ->    28
|*  |   |   |   |*  |   |   B           column  19 from    26 :   B                        ->    27
|*  |   |   |   |*  |   |   B           column  20 from    27 :   B                        ->    28
|*  |   |   |   |*  |   B               column  21 from    28 :   B                        ->    29
|*  |   |   |   |*  A                   column  22 from    29 :   A                        ->    30
|*  |   |   |   B                       column  23 from    30 :   B                        ->    31
|*  |   |   A                           column  24 from    31 :   A                        ->    32
|*  |   C                               column  25 from    32 :   C                        ->    33
|*  |   t                               column  26 from    33 :   BB                       ->    35
|*  |   |   B                           column  26 from    33 :   B                        ->    34
|*  |   |   B                           column  27 from    34 :   B                        ->    35


Node starting at position 23 and ending at 31 is a nested Rules.t.
Node starting at position 24 and ending at 30 is a nested Rules.t.
Node starting at position 25 and ending at 29 is a nested Rules.t.
Node starting at position 26 and ending at 28 is a nested Rules.t.
```

## Installation :

Create a virtual environment if desired, and do "pip install sqlite3_parser".
If you don't use "uv" package manager ("pip install sqlite3_parser") you might need to make sure clang compiler is available and in the path.

To make it available, pick the right "clang+llvm" for your platform at https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/releases/tag/llvmorg-18.1.8 or other version, and extract it to a folder.
Then add this folder (containing clang++ binary) to the path. You're up to go!

modules.name: parser
documentation:
Regroup major reference of the parsing library.

* AutoCalculatingToken provide a valid dataclass based ClassicalTokenProtocol implementation,
    requiring just token_type, content, start_position, stop_position, data, and the preceding token (previous).
* parse perform a simple non cached and non split parse, returning a ClassicTree.
* parse_to_tree_with_cache does the same but with cache, and expecting token indexes at which query should be split.
* CachingParser allows to use multiple diffeernt configurations at the same cache, including different cache file.
* BaseToken is a subclassable token that is a valid ClassicalTokenProtocol implementation,
    requiring to set manually each of its attributes.
* ScriptContext is required for splitting and parsing with CachingParser or parse_to_tree_with_cache.
    Each different script has a different ScriptContext.

modules.name: parser.script_context
documentation: Implement ScriptContext, that carry the informations and some utlities used by different part of the parsing chain.

methods.name: parser.script_context.ScriptContext.__reduce__
documentation: Doesn't store indexes.

methods.name: parser.script_context.ScriptContext.build_positions_equivalences
documentation: Build the dictionnaries used to tell if two positions are only separated by ignored tokens and are semantically equivalent.

methods.name: parser.script_context.ScriptContext.get_token_index_from_start_position
documentation: Retrieve the index of a token from its start position.

methods.name: parser.script_context.ScriptContext.get_token_index_from_stop_position
documentation: Retrieve the index of a token from its stop position.

Carry the informations and some utlities used by different part of the parsing chain.

It is expected that each character is covered by one and only one token (tokens are contiguous).

modules.name: parser.memoize
documentation: Cut script into statements, convert them into tree and cache the result for next call, before returning merged tree.

functions.name: parser.memoize.split_query
documentation:
Split the given script into chunks. Token in script_context MUST be ClassicalToken compatible tokens.

```
param script_context

script_context whose content will be split.

param split_index

character indexes at which the script will be split.
The tokens lists will be split at the matching place.

return

a list of triplets for each section, composed of the script chunk, the tokens chunk, the active tokens chunks.
```

Each chunk contains a whitespace free and non whitespace free Token list chunks, and a script chunk.
The whitespaces between two chunks TODO.

functions.name: parser.memoize.parse_to_tree_with_cache
documentation: Set cache size and parse a script returning FullParseResultType instance in one call. Must always be called with the same parameters during one execution.

classes.name: parser.memoize.ExcludedTokenError
documentation: The merge roor didn't include one ore more token from the root.

classes.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.__init__
documentation:
Set cache parameters.

```
param cache_path

Path | str | None = None: If specified, the cache will be saved on disk            and persist between executions. Otherwise the cache will stay in memory.

param shared_cache_size

int = 1_000_000: limit the total size of cached script to this amount in character.
```

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.cache_tree_and_subtree
documentation: Store in cache the parsed tree.

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.memoized_parse_chunk
documentation: Return the Tree obtained from parsing the chunk, parsing only if the result is not in cache.

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.__call__
documentation:
Produce the parse Tree and all the other informations from a string, using memoization and cutting the string into smaller chunks for faster parsing.

```
param merge_root_path

list[int] | None = None: If specified, for each tree,            the children of the node at this path will be all merged under the node at this path of the first tree.            Otherwise, no merged tree will be returned.
```

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.script_to_trees
documentation: Get deepcopy of script context and trees.

methods.name: parser.memoize.CachingParser.get_merged_tree
documentation: pop_last_empty_if_s_non_empty should be False when the last active token isn't necessarily a separator.

Split the scripts into chunks, parse them separately and memoize the result.

So that :
    * little change on a big script doesn't result in reparsing it entirely.
    * super linear parsing time of the parser doesn't become a problem.

methods.name: parser.tree.classic_tree.ClassicTree.lines
documentation:
Match if the given line contains part of node's content, or is adjacent to a newline part of the node's content.

If two lines are given, the start and endline should be exactly these two numbers.

If there is no columns and lines coordinates, fallback on positions.
The difference is that if it is a tuple (not a slice),             the content should be between caracters at the indexes (but doesn't have to up to those bounds).

methods.name: parser.tree.classic_tree.ClassicTree.duck_copy
documentation:
Construct a ClassicTree based tree from a Tree (or subclass) based tree.

There are no check that the tokens have line and column attribute.
Tree with columns and lines, and token with data attribute.

documentation: Different contents have different hashs.

documentation: Index of the first character of the token in the input.

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenProtocol.stop_position
documentation: Index of the last character of the token + 1 in the input.

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenProtocol.content
documentation: String content of the token.

A token must implement all these attributes in order to be used with the library.

However, if there is no special handling of lines and this information can be discarded, there is no need         to implement start_line, stop_line, start_column, and stop_column, the implementation in this class can be used.
extended_token_type also doesn't need to be implemented if not needed.

classes.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenWithPositionProtocol

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenWithPositionProtocol.start_line
documentation: Number of \n characters before the first character if the token.

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenWithPositionProtocol.stop_line
documentation: Start line increased of the number of \n in the token.

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenWithPositionProtocol.start_column
documentation:
Number of non newline characters between the first character of this token and the first \n before it.

So the first character of a line, right after a \n or the first character of a file, has a start_column of 0.

methods.name: parser.tree.protocols.TokenWithPositionProtocol.stop_column
documentation:
Number of non newline characters between the last character of this token and the first 
 before it.

Therefore, if a token ends with a 
 and the previous character is not a 
, stop_column will be nonzero.
Token that has columns and lines positions.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.ContentMatch.get_matches_against
documentation:
Return the list of successful matches as a MatchItem list.

If value is of type Pattern, it it used in the regex function as pattern.
If value starts and stops with a foward slash "/", value[1:-1] is used as a regex pattern.
Otherwise the string is compared litterally.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.DynamicMatchAttributeHelper.__init__
documentation:
Create the helper and set its test to match_test.

match_test will often maintain a closure on the Tree node.
However theymight not if the value of the test only depends on the other.
Or if they consider the tree frozen, they could pick one of several match_test function they have and use it.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.DynamicMatchAttributeHelper.__eq__
documentation: Use the provided test function with the other value as only argument, and return directly its return value.

documentation: Names are not stored (neither bound to match_test).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.DynamicMatchAttributeHelper.__getitem__

documentation: Build a Predicate instance from a predicate callable, possibly a function, possibly with a closure.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.Predicate.__getattr__
documentation:
Implement the behavior desired for pattern matching.

The return value in these cases are a new Predicate object with its quantifier set to a            value different from none, which can be used immediately for comparison.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.Predicate.__eq__
documentation: Check that all or any of the contained object of other verify the predicate.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree_auxiliary.Predicate.__call__
documentation: Equivalent to self.predicate(x).

Construct a predicate object.

To be used in structural pattern matching as predicate.all or predicate.any.
Example

```
def has_even_size(s: str) -> bool:
    return not len(s) % 2
predicate = Predicate(has_even_size)
match tree:
    case Tree(iterable_attribute=predicate.all):
        print(f"{tree} matched. All of its iterable_attribute element verified predicate.")
```

modules.name: parser.providers.lark
documentation:
Implement Lark parser provider.

Instanciate the provider with a grammar, and call parse on a script_context and active token list to get a ClassicTree.

functions.name: parser.providers.lark.simple_parse_lark
documentation:
Produce a ClassicTree based AST from a token list.

Lark earley parser is the default used parser.

```
param full_tokens_list

A list or iterable of the tokens obtained by tokenizing (lexing/scanning) the input (often text).
The tokens should satisfie the ClassicalTokenProtocol.
AutoCalculatingToken class can be used as it does satisfie this protocol.
Ignored tokens (if there are) should be included in the iterable.
The token_type field of each token should be part of an Enum representing the set of Terminals.
The value of each members of this Enum should match a Terminal in the grammar.
The terminals should be declared in the grammar (Lark grammar) using:

%declare TerminalA TerminalB

Ignored tokens can be declared but doesn't have to.

param ignored_tokens

A frozenset of every members of the Terminals Enum that are considered ignored (like whitespaces).
Tokens having one of these as token_type will be stripped out when computing active_tokens_list             and will not be passed to Lark parser.
A list of such Enum members can be converted to frozenset just by calling frozenset(ignored_list).

param rules_enum

An Enum.
Every rules used in grammar must have a matching rules_enum member whose value is exactly the lowercase rule name.

param grammar

A Lark context-free grammar,         possibly ambiguous.
The start symbol is named "start" and should therefore have a production rule in the gramamr,             and a matching rules_enum member value should be the str "start".
The grammar should not define any token.
The rules should not contain any string_literal nor regexp_literal.
```

```
return

The ClassicTree of the parse.
Element attribute will be either a Terminal Enum member, a rules_enum member, or None.
```

Given
: - an iterable of all (active and ignored) tokens
  - the ignored tokens types (terminals) frozenset
  - the rules Enumeration
  - and the lark grammar (with matching terminals name and ruled name)

produce a ClassicTree based AST.

methods.name: sqlite3_parser.sqlite_tree.SQLiteTree.regexable_left_individual_ddash_comments
documentation:
Case sensitive.

\n is appended at the end of each individual comment content.
Terminate your pattern with it to make it a fullmatch, as there is only one \n in each content.

classes.name: sqlite3_parser.rules.RulesTypes
documentation:
Their value is a string containing the name of each rule exactly the way it appear in the grammar.

Their name is just an upcased version with the exact same letters.

modules.name: sqlite3_parser.complete_binding
documentation: Produce a token list from a str.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.complete_binding.generate_ffi
documentation:
Produce the ffi for the shared library built from tokenizer.c++.

The C/C++ parsing time can be significant for small inputs.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.complete_binding.init_module
documentation:
Init CFFI and stores state in a Globals object.

Other context objects can be supplied using g arguments in sqlite3_complete_Binding.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.complete_binding.sqlite3_complete_Binding
documentation: Return a list of tuple containing position, length, TokenType and ETokenType.

methods.name: sqlite3_parser.complete_binding.Globals.__init__
documentation: Init CFFI and stores state in a Globals object.

modules.name: sqlite3_parser.__main__
documentation: Parse file at the path provided in the command line and print its tree.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.__main__.main
documentation: Parse the (single) file at the path provided as command line argument, to obtain a remerged tree.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.__main__.run
documentation: Run main with command line arguments.

modules.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer
documentation: Helper module for tests or quickly using the grammar.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.get_script_context
documentation: Obtain script_context, unicode_offsets and encoded_str from a str, and optionally valid_split_indexes.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.simple_parse
documentation: Parse the given script.

classes.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.SQLiteParser

methods.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.SQLiteParser.__init__
documentation: Pass None as cache_path to use in memory cache. Otherwise pass path to cache file to user.

methods.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.SQLiteParser.parse
documentation: Parse the given script, cutting it in chuck, and using memoization with a cache.
Parser with a cache.

classes.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.SQLiteFullParseResultType

class_variables.name: sqlite3_parser.parser_and_memoizer.SQLiteFullParseResultType.rebuilt_parse_tree_sc
documentation: Script context
Contain all the results obtained during treatment of a string, including parse tree(s).

modules.name: sqlite3_parser.encoding
documentation: Get encoded string and corresponding bytes-str offset and compensation fucntion.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.encoding.get_unicode_offsets_and_encoded_str
documentation: Count each extra byte by appending the index several times to the returned list.

functions.name: sqlite3_parser.encoding.compensate_offset
documentation: Return the corresponding index in the decoded string from the one in the encoded one.

modules.name: sqlite3_parser.terminals
documentation: Define every token used by SQLite.

classes.name: sqlite3_parser.terminals.TokenType
documentation: Names follow those defined by sqlite.

classes.name: sqlite3_parser.terminals.ETokenType
documentation: Give extra informations on a token. Uses execution path of the tokenizer or any other information.

modules.name: parser.tree.tree
documentation: Implements the Tree class which is root, nodes and leaf of the Tree it consist.

functions.name: parser.tree.tree.duck_copy
documentation: Try to construct a cls based tree from a Tree (or subclass) tree.

classes.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__init__
documentation: tree_attributes contains everything that is shared at the tree-level.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__hash__
documentation: Hash entire tree, and the path from the root to the node.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.get_detached_subtree_copy
documentation: Get a standalone tree deep copied from the subtree rooted on this node.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__reduce__
documentation: NodeDataType and TreeDataType must be picklable type.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__deepcopy__
documentation: Deep copy the whole tree, and get returns the node at the same position in the tree than this one.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.token
documentation: Proxy checking that the value has been initialized before use.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.rule
documentation: Proxy checking that the value has been initialized before use.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.parent
documentation: Proxy checking that the value has been initialized before use.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.active_tokens_bounds
documentation: Proxy checking that the value has been initialized before use.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.enclosing_token_pair
documentation:
Return the first and last active token of this Tree (that can be the same).

If the tree doesn't have any, return a tuple containing two None and the previous Token.
If there is no previous token, and the tree is empty, return (None, None, 0).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.content_bounds
documentation: Get the bounds of this tree in the input text.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.tokens_bounds
documentation: Get the bounds of this tree in the full token list.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.start_position
documentation: Get the start position of this tree in the input text.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.stop_position
documentation: Get the stop position of this tree in the input text.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__len__
documentation: Get the length of the contained string, including ignored text as long as it is not at a boundary.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.content_length
documentation: Get the length of the contained string, including ignored text as long as it is not at a boundary.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.content
documentation: The text enclosed between active Tokens participating to this rule, or the text matched by this token.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.token_length
documentation: Total number of token in this Tree, active or not.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.token_content
documentation: Tokens, active or not, that are part of this tree.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.active_token_length
documentation: Total number of active token in this Tree.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.active_token_content
documentation: Active tokens that are part of this tree.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.is_no_match
documentation:
The rules made optionnal with a pair of [brackets] leaves a None in the tree when they do not match.

Return true if this Tree node is such optional that didn't match.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.is_token
documentation: Return true if this Tree node is a Token.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.is_rule
documentation: Return true if this Tree node is a rule.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.element
documentation: Return the type of the current node (Tree) : TokenType, RulesTypes or None.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.path
documentation: Position in the child of each ancestor to reach the node, starting from the root.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.root
documentation: Root of the tree of which this node is part.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.fullmatch_content
documentation: Use re.fullmatch.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.case_sensitive_fullmatch_content
documentation: Use re.fullmatch.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.regexable_content
documentation: Use re.fullmatch.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.case_sensitive_regexable_content
documentation: Use re.fullmatch.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.match_content
documentation: Use re.match.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.case_sensitive_match_content
documentation: Use re.match.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.search_content
documentation: Use re.search.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.case_sensitive_search_content
documentation: Use re.search.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree._list_depth_first_with_levels
documentation:
Yield the node and its nesting level, then its first child and its nesting level (n + 1), 
then its first child, and then goes to the deepest next child.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.depth_first_list_with_nexting_levels
documentation: List all nodes depth first as tuples with their respective nesting levels.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.total_childs_number
documentation: Recursively get the total child number, including self.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.breadthfirst_list
documentation: Return all the children from each depth level starting at the root, starting at the left every time.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.bottom_to_top_right_to_left_list
documentation: Return all the children from each depth level ending at the root, starting at the left every time.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.depth_first_right_to_left_list
documentation: Yield the node, then its last child, then its last child, and then goes to the deepest next child.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.depth_first_list
documentation: Yield the node, then its first child, then its first child, and then goes to the deepest next child.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__iter__
documentation: Yield the node, then its first child, then its first child, and then goes to the deepest next child.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.repr
documentation: Start and stop position, and rule name or TokenType are aligned. Also dislay the first four children.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.ancestors
documentation:
Return the list of all the ancestor.

Exclude the node on which this function was called, start with the direct parent and finish with the root.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.is_leaf
documentation: Return whether the node doesn't have any children.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.ancestor_rules_types
documentation: Set containing every different RulesTypes present in (direct and indirect) ancestors.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.descendants_elements
documentation: Set containing every different element present in (direct and indirect) descendants.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.has_unordered_ancestors_rulestypes
documentation:
Equal to value if the given RulesTarget exists in the order provided in ancestors.

Possibly skipping ancestors but using the order provided.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.has_unordered_elements_in_descendants
documentation: Match if element is present in descendant.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.ignored_token_content
documentation: List every ignored token in the subtree, as long as they are (immediately or not) between active tokens.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.ignored_content
documentation: Merged contained whitespaces, not only comments.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.left_ignored_token
documentation: Ignored tokens preceding the subtree, until the first active token.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.left_greedy_ignored_content
documentation: Merged contained whitespaces and all preceding whitespaces until next active token, not only comments.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.regexable_ignored_content
documentation: Match the provided regex or str against left_greedy_whitespace_content.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.match_total_children_number
documentation: Match against (min, max) or exact_value.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.match_direct_children_number
documentation: Match against (min, max) or exact_value.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.active_content
documentation: Contents of all descendant active token in order, join using the specified str.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.match_active_content
documentation: Merged by a space.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.lines
documentation:
Docstring possibly outdated.

Match if the given line contains part of node's content, or is adjacent to a newline part of the node's content.

If two positions are given, the startline and endline should be exactly these two numbers.

If there is no columns and lines coordinates, fallback on positions.
The difference is that if it is a tuple (not a slice),             the content should be between caracters at the indexes (but doesn't have to up to those bounds).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__eq__
documentation:
Compare two subtrees and return true if they have (probably) identical semantical roles, and the same content.

For instance, a statement subtrees from a programming language should compare equal to itself regardless
    of its neighbours and position among the parent syntax element (unless this element was changed).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.compare_trees_and_nodes_positions
documentation: Return True if the trees, the calling node, and their position in their respective trees are the same.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.compare_trees
documentation:
Return true if the whole trees the nodes belong to are equal.

Nodes themselves are not compared directly.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.compare_subtrees
documentation: Check if each node of two subtrees have the same content, shape, and the same element (Token or RulesTypes).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.compare_node
documentation: Check if two nodes have the same content (by checking (str) content, active_tokens_bounds and element).

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.positions_are_equivalent
documentation:
Return true if the two positions are only separated by whitespace, including (multiline or not) comments, or nothing.

Max for a and b are the length of the content, corresponding to the position after the last character.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.token_indexes_are_equivalent
documentation:
Return true if the two (full) token indexes are equivalents.

Max for a and b is the number of tokens (including ignored tokens) content,         corresponding to the position after the last token.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__bool__
documentation: Return tree if there is at least one token in the tree.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.get_nth_ancestor
documentation: Pass -1 to level to get the root.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__getitem__

type.name: (int | slice | tuple[int, ...] | list[int] | MatchItem[TerminalType, RulesType, TreeDataType, NodeDataType])

documentation:
Retrieve the n-th direct children when using an int as index.

When index is a slice behave like doing node.children[index].
A MatchItem can be used as index on a different Tree than the one it originates.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.apply_match_item_relative_to_node
documentation: Follow the path, and get the node or its matching content by default.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__contains__
documentation: Warning : can be very long.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.contains_node
documentation:
__contains__ when other (possibly contained) is a Tree node.

Return true if the possibly contaning node has a node N such that N == other.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.check
documentation: Perform checks on all nodes, raising on error, and return True.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.check_init
documentation: Perform some checks on the Tree to see if it seems ill formed.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.reload
documentation: After modifications on any node of the tree, call this method to discard caches.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__setattr__
documentation: Prevent accidental modification on a locked tree. __setattr__ = object.__setattr__ is your friend.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.__delattr__
documentation: Prevent accidental modification on a locked tree.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.add_children
documentation: Link a parent and a child node, updating parent's children and child's parent.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.update_from_leaves
documentation: Update the active token bounds.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree._update_from_leaves
documentation: For Tree that are Token, the active_tokens_bounds must be already set.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.process_tree_bottom_up
documentation: Apply a transformation such that it is never applied on a parent before it applied on the children.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.add_childs_to_dict
documentation:
For debugging purpose, add the children of every nodes of this tree as attributes of their parent.

They are named 0, 1, 2, etc...

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.pretty_line
documentation: Represent a single node without its hierarchy.

methods.name: parser.tree.tree.Tree.pretty
documentation:
Return a string representation of the whole subtree rooted on this node.

Each nesting level produces 4 characters indentation.

Implement the building block of a tree (a node), that can either be a rule or a token.

If you use regex matching, be sure to know :
* the difference between search, match, and fullmatch
* whether . matches \n
* that you need to wrap your str bewteen two / forward slash or it will be compared litterally.

classes.name: parser.tree.tree.PicklingTree
documentation: Tree structure without reference to parent used to pickle Tree.
